threats.title: P-104 Mechanical Seal Leak Causing Delays
threats.cause: Unknown (potential seal wear, misalignment, dry running, or flush plan issues)
recommended_actions: Inspect mechanical seal faces/O-rings and replace seal cartridge; verify correct materials and installation practices.
Check pump alignment, shaft runout, bearing condition, and vibration; correct any contributors to seal distress.
Verify seal support system (flush/plan, cooling, seal pot/barrier fluid level/pressure if applicable) and restore to spec to prevent dry running/overheating.
Implement enhanced monitoring (seal leak rate trend, vibration/temperature checks) and add an operator check/response procedure to minimize downtime.

threats.title: P-104 Mechanical Seal Leak Causing Delays
threats.cause: Unknown (not provided)
recommended_actions: Inspect mechanical seal faces, O-rings, and gland for wear/damage; replace seal cartridge if condition is suspect.
Verify seal support system (flush/plan, seal pot level/pressure, cooling) is operating to spec; clear any plugged flush lines/filters.
Check pump alignment, shaft runout, and bearing condition to rule out vibration-induced seal failure; correct alignment and repair bearings as needed.
Review operating conditions (suction pressure/NPSH, dry-run events, temperature, solids) and adjust procedures/setpoints to prevent seal upset.

threats.title: Pump P-200 bearing failure
recommended_actions: Implement vibration analysis and bearing temperature trending with alarm limits to detect early degradation.
Verify lubrication type/quantity and relubrication intervals; check for contamination (water/particles) and improve seals/breathers as needed.
Check pump-driver alignment and soft foot; correct base/grout issues and eliminate pipe strain to reduce bearing loads.
Inspect for hydraulic issues (cavitation, operating far from BEP); adjust operating conditions or impeller clearances to minimize vibration and radial load.

threats.title: HX-301 reduced efficiency due to fouling
threats.cause: Accumulation of deposits/scale on heat transfer surfaces (possible poor feedwater quality, inadequate filtration/chemical treatment, or operating outside design conditions).
recommended_actions: Trend key indicators (approach temperature, overall heat transfer coefficient/U-value, and pressure drop) and set alarms to detect fouling early.
Implement a cleaning plan (CIP/chemical clean or mechanical cleaning) based on fouling rate and measured performance degradation.
Review and improve upstream treatment/filtration and chemical dosing to reduce scaling/particulate deposition.
Verify operating conditions (flows, temperatures, velocities) are within design to minimize fouling and consider adding side-stream filtration if recurring.

threats.title: Pump P-101 mechanical seal leak
threats.cause: Likely mechanical seal wear/damage or improper seal flush/face lubrication (exact cause not yet confirmed).
recommended_actions: Verify leak source is the mechanical seal (not casing, gasket, or seal gland) and document leak rate (drips/min) and fluid identity.
Inspect/confirm seal support system: seal flush/plan line open and flowing, correct pressure/temperature, and no plugged or reversed flush lines; correct any abnormal conditions.
Check pump operating conditions (suction pressure/NPSH margin, deadheading/low flow, vibration) that can overheat the seal; correct operation to within design envelope.
Plan maintenance: monitor trend and schedule seal inspection/replacement at earliest practical opportunity; prepare spare seal and verify installation procedure/alignment before restart.

threats.title: C-301 high vibration and abnormal noise
threats.cause: Unknown; likely contributors include bearing wear/damage, rotor imbalance, misalignment, loose foundation/hold-downs, coupling issues, or lubrication problems.
recommended_actions: Verify vibration levels and spectrum (overall, 1X/2X, bearing fault frequencies) and trend against alarm limits; correlate with operating conditions (speed/load).
Inspect and correct mechanical contributors: check coupling alignment, soft-foot, hold-down bolt torque, base/foundation looseness, and piping strain.
Check lubrication system condition (oil level/quality, filters, pressure/temperature) and inspect bearings for overheating, wear, or damage; perform oil analysis if applicable.
If vibration/noise exceeds trip/alarm limits or is rapidly worsening, reduce load and schedule controlled shutdown for detailed inspection (coupling, rotor, bearings) to prevent secondary damage.

threats.title: Cooling Water Pump P-101 mechanical seal leak
threats.cause: Likely worn/damaged mechanical seal faces or O-rings; possible misalignment or seal flush issue
recommended_actions: Inspect seal area and confirm leak rate; check seal flush/pressure and shaft alignment
Plan seal repair/replace (seal faces, O-rings) and inspect shaft sleeve for scoring
Verify correct operating conditions (suction pressure/NPSH, vibration) to prevent repeat seal failure

threats.title: P-101 high vibration
threats.cause: Possible impeller imbalance, misalignment, looseness, bearing wear, or cavitation
recommended_actions: Verify operating conditions (flow/suction pressure) and check for cavitation; correct suction restrictions if found
Inspect and correct alignment/soft foot; check for looseness at base, coupling, and hold-down bolts
Collect vibration/bearing condition data and inspect bearings/impeller for wear or damage; repair/replace as needed

threats.title: Cooling Water Pump P-101 overheating
threats.cause: Likely inadequate cooling/lubrication, operation off-design (deadhead/low flow), bearing distress, or coupling/misalignment increasing load
recommended_actions: Verify pump is not deadheaded/low-flow; confirm discharge valve position and minimum flow/bypass is open as required
Check bearing temperature/lube oil or grease condition and verify cooling water/flush (if applicable) is flowing
Inspect coupling alignment and motor current/overload; stop and isolate pump if temperature continues to rise

threats.title: P-101 grinding noise (possible bearing/impeller damage) - UNDO_TEST_1773920856742 - UNDO_TEST_1773920972236 - UNDO_TEST_1773921084536
threats.cause: Likely bearing degradation, impeller-to-casing rub from misalignment, debris ingestion, or inadequate lubrication

threats.title: P-101 pump grinding noise and seal leak
threats.failure_mode: Mechanical seal failure / bearing wear (abnormal noise and leakage)
threats.cause: Likely worn/damaged mechanical seal and/or bearing degradation; possible misalignment, inadequate lubrication, or cavitation
recommended_actions: Inspect seal area for leak rate and condition; check flush/plan (if applicable) and verify correct seal support conditions
Check bearing condition (vibration/temperature), coupling alignment, and lubrication; investigate cavitation indicators (suction pressure, strainer blockage)
Plan controlled shutdown to replace/repair mechanical seal and inspect bearings/shaft sleeve; verify runout before restart

threats.title: Extruder overheating in Building A
threats.cause: Unknown (possible cooling failure, excessive load, heater/control fault, blocked vents, low lubricant)
recommended_actions: Verify actual temperatures vs setpoints; check heater control loops/thermocouples for drift or failure
Inspect cooling system (fans/water flow), vents/filters, and ensure adequate airflow and no blockages
Reduce load or stop if temperature continues to rise; check lubrication and mechanical binding before restart

threats.title: Compressor making strange noise
threats.cause: Unknown (possible bearing wear, rotor contact, coupling misalignment, or insufficient lubrication)
recommended_actions: Inspect compressor for abnormal vibration/noise source (bearings, coupling alignment, looseness) and check oil level/condition
Trend vibration and temperature; compare to baseline and set alarm limits
If noise escalates or vibration exceeds limits, reduce load and schedule controlled shutdown for internal inspection

threats.title: P-102 grinding noise
threats.failure_mode: Abnormal noise (grinding) / bearing or internal rubbing
threats.cause: Likely bearing wear, impeller-to-casing rub, or cavitation (not yet confirmed)
recommended_actions: Check bearing condition and lubrication; verify coupling alignment and listen/measure vibration
Inspect for cavitation (suction pressure/NPSH, suction strainer blockage, valve positions) and correct suction conditions
If noise persists, plan controlled shutdown to inspect impeller/wear rings for rub damage

threats.title: P-102 grinding noise and vibration
threats.cause: Possible bearing wear/damage leading to abnormal noise and vibration
recommended_actions: Inspect bearing condition (temperature, lubrication state, vibration spectrum) and verify no rub/contact is occurring
Check alignment, coupling condition, and mounting/soft-foot; correct any issues found
Plan controlled shutdown to replace bearings if noise/vibration persists or increases

threats.title: Pump P-101 leak in cooling water system
threats.cause: Likely mechanical seal or gasket degradation/loosened fittings causing external leakage
recommended_actions: Inspect leak source (mechanical seal, casing gasket, flange/fitting connections) and document leak rate/extent
Verify pump suction/discharge pressures and check seal flush/plan (if applicable); repair/replace seal or gaskets and re-torque flanges to spec
Check for secondary damage (bearing contamination, coupling alignment) and clean up any standing water to prevent slip/electrical hazards

threats.title: Cooling pump P-101 leak
threats.cause: Likely mechanical seal or gasket degradation/wear causing leakage
recommended_actions: Inspect leak source (mechanical seal, gasket, casing drain/vent, flanges) and quantify leak rate
Plan seal/gasket replacement and verify alignment/shaft condition to prevent repeat seal failure
Check bearing condition and vibration to rule out misalignment/bearing wear contributing to seal leak

threats.title: Pump making abnormal noise
threats.cause: Likely bearing wear or inadequate lubrication (also possible cavitation or misalignment)
recommended_actions: Inspect pump bearings and lubrication condition; check for overheating at bearing housings
Check alignment/coupling and verify suction conditions to rule out cavitation (NPSH, blocked strainer, low level)
Trend vibration/noise and schedule repair or controlled shutdown if noise increases

threats.title: Compressor Overheating
threats.cause: Insufficient cooling or lubrication, high discharge pressure, fouled cooler/filter, or instrumentation/control issue causing elevated operating temperature
recommended_actions: Verify suction/discharge pressures and temperatures; compare to normal operating envelope and trip/alarms
Check cooling system performance (cooler fouling, cooling water/air flow, fan operation) and lube oil level/condition
Inspect/clean inlet filters and confirm anti-surge/recycle and control valves are operating correctly

threats.title: Valve leak in Section B
threats.cause: Degraded packing/seat or loose flange/gasket allowing leakage
recommended_actions: Verify leak source (packing vs body/bonnet vs flange) and quantify leak rate; isolate if escalating
Tighten packing gland/flange bolts per procedure if applicable; replace packing/gasket if needed
Inspect valve seat/stem for wear/corrosion and schedule repair/overhaul at next outage

threats.title: Pump making noise (possible bearing failure)
threats.cause: Unknown (possible worn/damaged bearings, inadequate lubrication, or cavitation)
recommended_actions: Inspect pump and motor bearings for wear/overheating and verify lubrication condition/level
Check alignment/coupling and verify suction conditions (NPSH, blockage, air ingress) to rule out cavitation

threats.title: Extruder Overheating
threats.cause: Possible cooling system issue, heater/control loop malfunction, excessive load, or poor ventilation
recommended_actions: Verify barrel/gearbox temperature readings and confirm sensor accuracy (check for faulty thermocouple/RTD)
Inspect cooling (fans, water/oil flow, heat exchanger) and clear any blockages; confirm setpoints and controller output
Check for mechanical overload (material jam, screw wear, bearing drag) and reduce load until temperatures stabilize

threats.title: Extruder overheating
threats.cause: Possible cooling system malfunction, excessive load, heater control fault, or ventilation blockage
recommended_actions: Verify barrel/gearbox cooling (flow/temperature), clean/clear vents and heat sinks, and confirm fans/pumps are operating
Check temperature control loop (thermocouples, SSR/heater outputs, PID settings) and compare indicated vs actual temperatures
Reduce throughput/load temporarily and inspect for mechanical drag (screw/bearing condition, material buildup) if temperatures remain high

threats.title: 00 1L01 lijn 90er Extruder operational issue
threats.cause: Unknown (insufficient symptom detail in latest message; prior context mentioned overheating)
recommended_actions: Confirm current symptom (temperature, alarms, throughput/quality changes) and capture operating conditions at time of issue
Inspect heaters/thermocouples and cooling water/air flow; verify temperature control loop operation
Check screw/barrel for abnormal load (torque, pressure) indicating blockage, contamination, or material feed issue

threats.title: Pump Leakage Issue
threats.impact: Potential pump failure leading to operational disruption
recommended_actions: Inspect seals and replace if necessary

recommended_actions: Monitor for changes in vibration levels

threats.title: Extruder Vibration Issue
threats.impact: The extruder is producing excessive vibration, which may lead to further mechanical issues.

Tighten mounting bolts and alignment adjustments

threats.title: Extruder Vibration Issue

recommended_actions: Clean the pump to remove fouling.

threats.title: Fouling Issue

threats.title: Fouling Issue

recommended_actions: Schedule cleaning of the pump to remove fouling.

threats.title: Failure mode: Short Circuit - Short Circuit

recommended_actions: Use calibration stickers with due dates

threats.title: Fouling Issue
recommended_actions: Monitor flow rate for significant changes

threats.title: Strainer Fouling
threats.impact: Strainer is fouled/blocked, potentially causing reduced flow and increased pressure drop across the strainer.
recommended_actions: Verify differential pressure across the strainer to confirm fouling severity.
Isolate and clean/flush the strainer element per procedure.
Inspect for upstream contamination source and address if recurring.
Restore to service and monitor DP/flow after cleanup.

threats.title: Strainer Fouling
threats.impact: Strainer is fouled/blocked, which can restrict flow and increase differential pressure.
recommended_actions: Verify inlet/outlet differential pressure across the strainer
Check upstream source for debris/contamination and address root cause

threats.title: Strainer Fouling
threats.impact: Strainer is fouled/blocked, which can restrict flow and increase differential pressure across the strainer.
recommended_actions: Check and trend differential pressure across the strainer to confirm fouling severity
Isolate and clean/replace strainer element per procedure
Inspect removed debris to identify source and prevent recurrence (upstream contamination, corrosion products, process upset)
Verify downstream flow/pressure returns to normal after cleaning

threats.title: Strainer Fouling
threats.impact: Restricted flow and increased differential pressure across the strainer; potential downstream starvation or process upset.
recommended_actions: Check and record differential pressure across the strainer.
Isolate and clean/replace strainer element as required.
Inspect debris type/source to prevent recurrence (upstream contamination check).
Verify downstream flow/pressure returns to normal after maintenance.

threats.title: Strainer Fouling
threats.impact: Restricted flow / increased differential pressure across strainer, potential downstream starvation and process upset.
recommended_actions: Verify differential pressure across 06 1M02 strainer and compare to alarm/cleaning limit
Isolate and clean/replace strainer element; inspect for damage
Capture debris type/source (rust, scale, product) and check upstream contamination control
Increase monitoring frequency until fouling rate is understood

threats.title: Strainer Fouling
threats.impact: Reduced flow / increased differential pressure across the strainer, potentially starving downstream equipment and causing process upsets.
recommended_actions: Verify differential pressure across the strainer and compare to normal/limits
Clean/flush or change out strainer element; inspect for debris type/source
Check upstream source for contamination and confirm blowdown/cleaning interval

recommended_actions.action: Test PM action - perform quarterly inspection

threats.title: 34  2M01 Extruder ZSE 60 - Screw Wear (Abrasive)
recommended_actions: If wear is confirmed above limits, prepare spares/repair plan (hardfacing/coated screw, barrel refurbishment) and review protective interlocks given safety-critical classification

recommended_actions.action: De-energize and perform immediate electrical inspection of heater circuits, motor/drive power cables, terminal blocks, junction boxes, and cabinet buswork for discoloration, loose terminations, damaged insulation, and contamination; torque-check all terminations to spec.

Verify protection and coordination: confirm correct fuse/breaker ratings, overload settings, ground-fault protection (if available), and that trips/alarms are functional; review recent trip logs from MCC/VFD/PLC.

threats.title: CW Pump P-101 - Custom XYZ Problem

recommended_actions.action: Perform drive and mechanical train checks: verify torque limiter/clutch (if installed), coupling condition/alignment, gearbox health indicators, and confirm VFD torque/current limits and protections are enabled and not bypassed.

threats.title: Issue: Lubrication reservoir low
threats.description:
Issue discovered during action execution.

Action: Improve lubrication

Details: Lubrication reservoir low
threats.cause: Need to order more lubricant

threats.title: Bearing failure due to excessive vibration on main pump P-101

recommended_actions.action: Verify and function-test all over-temperature protections: high-high barrel/melt temperature trips, emergency stop permissives, heater contactor drop-out, and any independent thermal cut-outs; document setpoints vs OEM limits.

Perform immediate temperature instrumentation validation: compare each zone thermocouple/RTD to a calibrated reference (or swap channels), check for loose wiring/ground faults/noise, and confirm controller PV matches independent measurement at steady state.
Inspect for fouling/plugging and confirm material feed consistency: check hopper, feeder calibration, venting, and die/filter condition; clean/remove buildup at the earliest safe opportunity

Schedule mechanical inspection at next safe stop: check screw/barrel wear profile, alignment, thrust bearing condition, and gearbox coupling; assess clearance increase drivers and correct misalignment/wear contributors
Conduct root-cause analysis focused on thermal control stability: validate heater bands, thermocouples, PID tuning, and cooling flow to prevent thermal runaway that can trigger seizure/over-torque

Implement immediate operating limits and interlocks review: verify torque, melt pressure, and motor load trips are enabled/set correctly; reduce throughput/torque setpoints until root cause is identified

Perform an urgent mechanical inspection of screw and barrel condition: verify actual running clearances, check for signs of screw-to-barrel contact, inspect screw elements/shaft for cracks or abnormal wear, and measure torque/vibration signatures at current load.
Implement immediate operating limits: enforce a temporary reduced throughput and strict torque/pressure interlocks with alarm escalation to prevent transient overload while investigation is underway

investigations.title: Investigation: P-101 grinding noise (possible bearing/impeller damage)
investigations.description:
Investigation initiated from threat report.

Asset: Cooling Water Pump P-101
Failure Mode: Abnormal noise (grinding) due to bearing failure or internal rubbing
Risk Level: Critical
Risk Score: 75

investigations.title: Investigation: P-101 grinding noise (possible bearing/impeller damage) - UNDO_TEST_1773920856742 - UNDO_TEST_1773920972236 - UNDO_TEST_1773921084536
investigations.description: Cooling Water Pump P-101 exhibits occasional grinding noise consistent with bearing distress and/or internal rubbing. Given safety-critical service and medium risk score, the most probable combined drivers are lubrication deficiency/contamination, bearing wear, misalignment leading to impeller rub, debris ingestion, and hydraulic instability (cavitation/recirculation).

investigations.title: Investigation: 34  2M01 Extruder ZSE 60 - Screw Breakage
investigations.description: Critical first-time screw breakage on safety-critical ZSE 60 extruder is most consistent with progressive abrasive wear leading to overload/fatigue, potentially exacerbated by process upsets (foreign bodies/high torque) and/or drive/electrical faults indicated by concurrent open observations (loose connection, short circuit).

investigations.title: Investigation: 34  2M01 Extruder ZSE 60 - Screw Breakage
investigations.description: Screw breakage on Extruder ZSE 60 is most likely driven by combined abnormal mechanical loading and thermal stress. Same-day open observations show clearance increase, material buildup/fouling, and overheating—each consistent with torque spikes, localized binding, and reduced mechanical strength leading to screw fracture.

investigations.title: Investigation: 34  2M01 Extruder ZSE 60 - Screw Breakage
investigations.description: Screw breakage on Extruder ZSE 60 is most likely driven by combined abnormal mechanical loading and thermal stress. Same-day open observations show clearance increase, material buildup/fouling, and overheating—each consistent with torque spikes, localized binding, and reduced mechanical strength leading to screw fracture.

cause_nodes.description: Material incompatibility with process fluid
Problem: P-101 grinding noise (possible bearing/impeller damage)
Failure Mode: Abnormal noise (grinding) due to bearing failure or internal rubbing
Potential Cause 1: Inadequate lubrication
Potential Cause 2: Contamination
Potential Cause 3: Misalignment
Potential Cause 4: Overloading
Hypothesis: Likely bearing degradation, impeller-to-casing rub from misalignment, debris ingestion, or inadequate lubrication
Material incompatibility with process fluid

Inadequate lubrication (insufficient grease/oil quantity, wrong lubricant type/viscosity, over-greasing causing churning/heat) or lubricant contamination (water ingress, solids) leading to accelerated bearing wear and grinding noise.

Bearing degradation/failure due to fatigue, corrosion (water ingress), electrical fluting (VFD/stray currents), or prior operation with poor lubrication, resulting in metal-to-metal contact and grinding.

Pump-to-motor misalignment and/or soft foot, pipe strain, or distorted baseplate causing shaft deflection and impeller-to-casing (or wear ring) rubbing, producing grinding and secondary bearing overload.

Debris/foreign material ingestion (scale, corrosion products, marine growth, gasket fragments) causing impeller damage or intermittent impeller-to-casing contact, leading to grinding noise and imbalance.

Hydraulic instability such as cavitation (low NPSH, suction restrictions, hot water, low level) or internal recirculation from operating far off BEP, generating noise that can be mistaken for grinding and causing rapid bearing/seal wear.

Inadequate lubrication (insufficient grease/oil quantity, wrong lubricant type/viscosity, over-greasing causing churning/heat) or lubricant contamination (water ingress, solids) leading to accelerated bearing wear and grinding noise.

Bearing degradation/failure due to fatigue, corrosion (water ingress), electrical fluting (VFD/stray currents), or prior operation with poor lubrication, resulting in metal-to-metal contact and grinding.

Pump-to-motor misalignment and/or soft foot, pipe strain, or distorted baseplate causing shaft deflection and impeller-to-casing (or wear ring) rubbing, producing grinding and secondary bearing overload.

Inadequate lubrication (insufficient grease/oil quantity, wrong lubricant type/viscosity, over-greasing causing churning/heat) or lubricant contamination (water ingress, solids) leading to accelerated bearing wear and grinding noise.

Bearing degradation/failure due to fatigue, corrosion (water ingress), electrical fluting (VFD/stray currents), or prior operation with poor lubrication, resulting in metal-to-metal contact and grinding.

Pump-to-motor misalignment and/or soft foot, pipe strain, or distorted baseplate causing shaft deflection and impeller-to-casing (or wear ring) rubbing, producing grinding and secondary bearing overload.

Debris/foreign material ingestion (scale, corrosion products, marine growth, gasket fragments) causing impeller damage or intermittent impeller-to-casing contact, leading to grinding noise and imbalance.

Hydraulic instability such as cavitation (low NPSH, suction restrictions, hot water, low level) or internal recirculation from operating far off BEP, generating noise that can be mistaken for grinding and causing rapid bearing/seal wear.
Progressive abrasive screw wear reduced screw root section and altered clearances, increasing stress concentration and torque demand until fatigue fracture or sudden overload breakage occurred.
Process upset causing excessive torque (e.g., foreign object/metal contamination, agglomerates, cold feed, blocked die/vent) leading to instantaneous overload and screw fracture.

Drive/electrical integrity issue (e.g., intermittent loose power/control connection or short-circuit-related transient) causing abnormal motor behavior, torque pulsations, or loss of protective functions, contributing to mechanical overload and screw failure.

Incorrect assembly/maintenance condition during prior screw work (e.g., improper torque on coupling/spline, incorrect element sequencing, misalignment, inadequate preload) creating stress risers and cyclic bending leading to early fatigue breakage.
Design/protection inadequacy: torque limiting/clutch or drive torque limit setpoints not present, not tuned, or ineffective, allowing torque to exceed screw mechanical limits during upsets.
Material buildup/fouling causing partial blockage and screw/barrel binding, leading to severe torque spikes and cyclic bending/torsional overload until the screw fractures.

Clearance increase (wear/misalignment of screw elements, barrel, gearbox thrust system, or bearings) causing eccentric rotation and cyclic bending loads; reduces load-carrying capability and promotes fatigue crack initiation leading to screw breakage.

Overheating/thermal runaway causing localized softening/creep of screw material and loss of mechanical strength; combined with high torque, results in torsional shear or accelerated fatigue leading to breakage.
Foreign object ingress or hard contamination (metal, stones, oversized pellets) causing instantaneous jam or impact loading on screw elements leading to brittle fracture or torsional overload.

Operation outside design envelope (excess feed rate, high viscosity/low melt temperature, improper screw speed, prolonged running at high torque) resulting in sustained over-torque and cumulative fatigue damage leading to screw failure.
Material buildup/fouling causing partial blockage and screw/barrel binding, leading to severe torque spikes and cyclic bending/torsional overload until the screw fractures.

Clearance increase (wear/misalignment of screw elements, barrel, gearbox thrust system, or bearings) causing eccentric rotation and cyclic bending loads; reduces load-carrying capability and promotes fatigue crack initiation leading to screw breakage.

Overheating/thermal runaway causing localized softening/creep of screw material and loss of mechanical strength; combined with high torque, results in torsional shear or accelerated fatigue leading to breakage.
Foreign object ingress or hard contamination (metal, stones, oversized pellets) causing instantaneous jam or impact loading on screw elements leading to brittle fracture or torsional overload.

Operation outside design envelope (excess feed rate, high viscosity/low melt temperature, improper screw speed, prolonged running at high torque) resulting in sustained over-torque and cumulative fatigue damage leading to screw failure.

action_items.description: Replace mechanical seal with compatible material
Replace mechanical seal with compatible material
Replace damaged bearing and check alignment

Perform drive and mechanical train checks: verify torque limiter/clutch (if installed), coupling condition/alignment, gearbox health indicators, and confirm VFD torque/current limits and protections are enabled and not bypassed.
Inspect for fouling/plugging and confirm material feed consistency: check hopper, feeder calibration, venting, and die/filter condition; clean/remove buildup at the earliest safe opportunity

Schedule mechanical inspection at next safe stop: check screw/barrel wear profile, alignment, thrust bearing condition, and gearbox coupling; assess clearance increase drivers and correct misalignment/wear contributors
Conduct root-cause analysis focused on thermal control stability: validate heater bands, thermocouples, PID tuning, and cooling flow to prevent thermal runaway that can trigger seizure/over-torque

Implement immediate operating limits and interlocks review: verify torque, melt pressure, and motor load trips are enabled/set correctly; reduce throughput/torque setpoints until root cause is identified
